fix.description: Full-screen capture per your note. Same fix: meals are AI-named at build (POST /ai/name-meals) and persist into the plan; the plan-ready Plan Details now reads real dish names - 'Turkey Oats Bowl / Chicken Rice Plate / Beef Rice Plate' - never a bare ingredient or slot label. Shown in full plan-ready context.

previousFix.description: Root cause: the plan-ready Weekly progression card rendered workouts only - the plan's meals were absent. Fix: WeeklyProgression gains a meals block (diet-pink chip) under the day tables: 'Same every day' shows one Every-day list, 'Varies by day' shows per-day lists, each meal with its ingredient-summed approximate kcal, computed from the saved diet payload. Evidence: Week 1 view showing Upper/Lower day tables followed by Meals this week - Same every day - Breakfast ~233 kcal / Lunch ~248 kcal.

history.note: this says oats which is not the meal name

previousFix.description: Root cause: the weekly-progression meals block rendered m.name, which for slot-named meals is just 'Breakfast'/'Lunch'/'Dinner' (the prior fix's verification literally showed 'Breakfast ~233 / Lunch ~248'). Fix: new mealDisplayName() shows the real dish name when a meal has one, else derives a name from its biggest ingredients by kcal impact (e.g. 'Chicken Breast & Rice', or 'A, B +N' for 3+); used in BOTH the weekly-progression block and the Plan Details day preview. FE-only, tsc clean. Verified live in /dev/plan-builder?stage=overview: meals now read 'Oats ~233 / Chicken Breast ~248' instead of 'Breakfast / Lunch', 0 console errors.

history.note: mean name not ingredient name that you have

previousFix.description: Root cause: re-fix #1 derived the top INGREDIENT ('Oats'), which (you're right) reads as an ingredient, not a meal name. Fix: mealDisplayName now (1) prefers a real recipe title (accepted-suggestion meals carry one), (2) else the meal's own non-slot name, (3) else composes a DISH name from cleaned top ingredients -> 'Oats Bowl' (one main) or 'Chicken Breast & White Rice' (two+), never a bare ingredient or slot label. cleanIngredientName strips '(Cooked)'/prep words. Verified live in the plan-ready overview: meals read 'Oats Bowl / Chicken Breast Bowl'. NOTE: these are composed from ingredients because the meal carries no real name in the plan data — for richer, LLM-quality per-meal names everywhere (the deeper fix, same root as 522a's slot-named meals) I'd name meals at plan generation; say the word and I'll do that.

Root cause (4th look): the prior 3 fixes derived the name from ingredients client-side, so a hand-built/slot-named meal still read as an ingredient ('Oats Bowl'). Fix (shipped 8f60a1e6 + backend db119e5): CustomDietBuilder.handleContinue now calls POST /ai/name-meals for every slot-named/unnamed meal, giving each a real LLM dish name that persists into the saved plan; PlanReadyOverview's mealDisplayName prefers that name. Verified the endpoint live (returns 'Banana Peanut Oat Bowl', 'Chicken & Broccoli Rice Bowl', 'Palak Paneer Roti') and verified the built plan overview now reads 'Turkey Oats Bowl / Chicken Rice Plate / Beef Rice Plate' — real dish names, never a bare ingredient or slot label, on every build path.

issues.description: In this page also show how coach will reduce the calories over time and increase the workout/activity give challenge and increase the calories if on track plan this so this actually happens with user over the weeks also add meals cards if looks good and same for workouts if looks good

fix.description: Full-screen capture per your note. Same fix: animated 'How your coach adapts your plan' timeline on the plan-ready overview - 4 staggered steps (calories ease 2218->2133 kcal, training climbs, on-track 80%+ earns calories back + a challenge, off-week holds, never punitive). Shown full-screen above the weekly progression.

previousFix.description: Owner said 'plan and do it'. PHASE 1 of 3 shipped + verified: app/services/weekly_adaptation.py — a pure decision engine (taper at each week's projected weight via the existing macro_calculator; +75 kcal on-track bonus when adherence >=80% AND weight on pace, capped below TDEE; HOLD at last week's target when off-track or no logs, never a punitive cut; BMR x1.1 floor; maintenance at goal) + 15 rule tests, all passing on prod venv (sample: week 4 on-track 2491 -> 2566 kcal). It is INERT (no DB writes, no scheduler) = zero production effect yet. Investigation also corrected the plan's baseline: the LIVE daily target is UserHealthProfile.target_kcal (static since onboarding) — that's now the resolved write target (WeeklyMacroTarget is display-only). STAGED next: Phase 2 = weekly dry-run scheduler that Discord-logs would-be changes (still no writes); Phase 3 = live weekly write behind FEATURE_WEEKLY_ADAPTATION + FE read. Phased deliberately because going live changes every athlete's calorie target. Resolve to greenlight Phase 2, or mark not-fixed if you want it fully wired now. Plan: docs/WEEKLY_ADAPTATION_PLAN.md

history.note: or you can just showsome nice graphics of it happning win animation

previousFix.description: You chose (B): keep the date, show an honest note. Confirmed cause: at an aggressive goal pace the calorie taper is BMR-floored (can't safely eat little enough to lose that fast), so the SAFE weight projection ends short of the goal while the goal card implies it's reached — that mismatch is the 'not accurate'. WeeklyTrajectory now shows a calm note when the curve doesn't reach goal within the horizon: 'At a safe pace you'll reach ~X kg by week N - about M weeks to reach your G kg goal. Going faster would mean eating below a healthy minimum.' No note when the projection DOES reach goal (no false positive). Your date is kept. Verified live: a goal-reaching plan (78->72) shows no note; an aggressive 78->55 plan shows '~66.9 kg by week 13 - about 28 weeks to reach your 55 kg goal', goal line correctly below the curve. tsc clean. (The Phase-1 weekly-adaptation engine from the earlier tick stands; the dry-run scheduler + live target writes remain staged.)

Owner's latest direction: 'just show nice graphics of it happening with animation' (deferring live weekly calorie writes for every athlete). Fix: new CoachAdaptationCard on the plan-ready overview — a calm animated vertical timeline (connector line draws in, 4 steps stagger up; reduced-motion static) telling the adaptation story with concrete numbers: (1) calories ease from this plan's real start to end kcal as you lean out, (2) training/activity climbs week over week, (3) on-track 80%+ earns calories back + a fresh challenge, (4) an off week holds steady, never a punitive cut. Placed right after the weekly trajectory so the chart (what) is followed by the narrative (how).

fix.description: Full-screen capture of the coach chat per your note (showing the animation context). history.note: Should look rich and badass this look plain and less detailed

previousFix.description: Owner: 'plan and do 3 options and you choose best one'. The coach chat had no character (just a name + 'Personal Coach' = the robot feeling). I prototyped 3 styles on the dark chat at header+FAB sizes -> (A) illustrated bust, (B) calm mascot blob, (C) monogram aura -> and CHOSE A: a drawn human coach (warm, no robot, readable at 40px and 120px); the mascot reads as a generic app blob and the monogram is just a letter. Built CoachAvatar (inline SVG, no external assets): a head-and-shoulders coach drawn deterministically per coach name (skin/hair/accent/hairstyle/optional beard, so every roster coach is a distinct person), with calm idle animation (slow breathing + occasional blink) that respects reduced-motion. Wired into the chat header. Verified live on light AND dark; 'Atlas' vs 'Rohan' render as different people; 0 console errors. Remaining surfaces (quick follow-ups): message name-chip, floating coach button, CoachPreview. If you'd rather use hand-drawn Lottie art per coach, CoachAvatar is the drop-in swap point (spec: docs/COACH_CHARACTER_ASSET_SPEC.md).

history.note: show.the complete screenshot where we also have the animation etc

previousFix.description: Owner: 'plan and do 3 options, you choose the best'; reopen: the drawn coach 'looks plain and less detailed', wants 'rich and badass'. Root cause: portraits came from a semi-flat/soft-studio prompt that read flat. I generated 3 rich style variants for the test coach — (A) painterly digital painting, (B) stylized 3D render, (C) bold editorial illustration — and CHOSE C: it reads most clearly as a DRAWN character (no robot/stock feel), is the boldest and most detailed, and stays premium (no neon/grunge). Regenerated all 13 active roster coaches in this style (backend generator 54a309b; OpenAI used only for character creation per your constraint). Image 1: the full rich portrait. Image 2: it live in the coach-chat header (drawn Rohan in the animated halo).

fix.description: Reworked PostPlanTour into a subscription-focused page. (1) Coach profile up top: the assigned coach's rich illustrated portrait + name in a live teal-to-purple halo. (2) What it costs after the trial: the 30-days-free card now shows 'After your free month 499/mo' + the founder offer (49 first month, cancel anytime, quarterly/half-yearly save more). (3) Buy from the start: a 'Choose a plan now' CTA routes straight to /choose-plan. (4) Animations: the feature icons spring in (calm). Subscription blocks gated on FEATURE_SUBSCRIPTION_GATE (prod); canonical pricing/live plans stay on /choose-plan. Image 1 = top (coach + plan + features), image 2 = trial card with pricing + CTAs.

fix.description: Re-published with a full-screen capture per your note. Same fix: the goal-reached subline is now full-contrast with a soft text-shadow so it stays legible over the blue fill, plus a reduced-motion-safe confetti burst + goal sound on the false-to-true goal transition. Shot: /nutrition with Water at 100% 'Goal reached - nicely done', in full context.

previousFix.description: Root cause: the goal-reached subline used text-muted-foreground and washed out once the blue water fill rose behind it; nothing marked the moment the goal was hit. Fix: full-contrast subline (text-foreground/90 + soft text-shadow on the overlay) so the % and 'Goal reached' stay legible over both empty tank and blue fill, plus a contained reduced-motion-safe confetti burst + goal sound fired once on the false-to-true goal transition.

issues.description: Meals images should be there use openai to generate images that can be used as place holder untill real are generated generate around 15-20 for such use.

fix.description: Generated a one-time pool of 18 appetizing plated-meal photos via OpenAI (bounded one-off, honoring the 'limit OpenAI use' rule — not per-request gen), served from catalog-images/meal/ph<n>.png. AsyncImage now shows one of these (picked deterministically by meal id) for any meal with no real image yet, instead of the bare category icon, and swaps to the real image the instant the worker's image_ready fires. Screenshot shows the no-image state from your report now rendering appetizing placeholders (smoothie bowl, shrimp skewers, paneer, wrap). Note: secure_filename on the serve route rejects a leading underscore, so the pool uses ph<n>.png.

issues.description: Send the image to openai and alow this feature for pro plan user

fix.description: Built pro-gated photo meal logging. Add Custom Meal now has a 'Snap or attach a photo (PRO)' button: the photo is sent to a new backend endpoint POST /ai/analyze-meal-photo (backend 8923ee7) which is pro-gated (active subscription required, else 403 -> upgrade), runs OpenAI gpt-4o-mini vision at 'low' detail, and returns a one-line meal description that prefills the field for the user to review + log. Cost is bounded per your 'limit OpenAI use' rule: pro-only + cheap model + a 25/day per-user cap. Verified end-to-end live: uploaded a meal photo, vision returned 'Grilled chicken breast (150g), steamed broccoli (100g), and white rice (150g)' which prefilled the description. Image 1: result, image 2: the PRO entry point.

issues.reviewReason: Part 1 DONE + pushed (b2b87867): the level-up/milestone overlay and the 'Badge earned' toast were firing on top of each other (your screenshot). Added a shared celebration bus — a full-screen overlay now marks it busy while on screen and the badge toast defers until it's idle, so they SEQUENCE (overlay first, then toast) instead of colliding. App-wide background tracking already works (LevelUpWatcher fires on any page). Caught + fixed an app crash I introduced while doing this. Couldn't stage a live side-by-side capture: the QA account's gamification overview endpoint is erroring (no level/badge data to trigger the overlay) — could you confirm on-device that level-up + badge no longer overlap? Part 2 (beautiful badge icons via OpenAI) is the remaining half — it's a ~one-time batch of badge images; I'll generate + wire those next. Reply to send it back as a normal fix.

previousFix.description: Intro carousel no longer needs three taps. The 3 pillar slides (Nourish / Train / Progress) now AUTO-CYCLE in a continuous loop (~3s each), and the CTA is a single 'Get Started' that proceeds at any point — instead of tapping 'Continue' through all three. Swipe still browses (wraps both ways) and the dots jump to a slide; auto-advance pauses while dragging. Verified live on /dev/intro: sampled the loop NOURISH -> TRAIN -> PROGRESS and confirmed one Get Started button. Reduced-motion safe.

previousFix.description: Root cause: tapping 'I'll build it myself' kicks off an eager save + builder prefetch (the busy window), but the screen only dimmed the cards (opacity-60) with no clear response — so the tap felt unanswered. Fix: the instant a path is chosen, the choice cards are replaced by a clear loading state ('Setting up your builder...' spinner for self-build, 'Coach is setting up your plan...' for coach) — immediate feedback that the next page is loading, instead of a dead delay. Verified on a new /dev/plan-choice harness: tapping the card swaps straight to the spinner view. tsc clean.

previousFix.description: Reworked the coach-build 'Your Goal' page (Screen9_CoachPreview). It was a plain icon-plate; now it LEADS with the assigned coach's rich drawn portrait (the 206f art) in a living teal-to-purple halo that springs in, with the greeting + your goal/nutrition details below, and the CTA now reads '[Coach], build my plan' (e.g. 'Rohan, build my plan') with a sparkle. The animated 'finalizing plan' page you described already exists (PlanBuildingScreen — the build choreography that plays until the plan is ready; preview at /dev/finalize-preview). Verified on a new /dev/coach-preview harness. Note: I used a circular halo portrait rather than the exact meal-card rectangular 'top image, blurred bottom' treatment — say the word if you want that exact framing and I'll swap it in.

Root cause: the plan-ready 'Plan Details > Meals' list mapped previewDietDay.meals directly (unfiltered), so an unfilled slot (0 ingredients) rendered as 'Dinner ~0 kcal' — unlike the weekly-progression list + the save path, which both strip empty meals. Fix: previewDietDay now filters out meals with no ingredients at the source, so a ~0 kcal empty slot never shows (and if a day has only empty slots, the existing 'No meals added yet' note shows). Verified on /dev/plan-builder?stage=overview (its seed has an empty dinner): Meals now reads just 'Oats Bowl / Chicken Breast Bowl' — the Dinner ~0 kcal row is gone.
